task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay and don’t want it to sound awkward. Can you draft a couple of versions for me, maybe one more polished and one more plain, so I can pick the better fit?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are two short options you can use, one a bit more polished and one more plain.

**Polished**
> Hi [Client Name],  
> I wanted to let you know there will be a short delay in getting this to you. I’m working through it and will send it as soon as possible. Thanks for your patience.

**Plain**
> Hi [Client Name],  
> Just a quick note to say there’s a delay on this end. I’m on it and will get it to you as soon as I can. Thanks for understanding.

If you want, I can also make them:
- a little more formal
- warmer/more apologetic
- firmer and more businesslike

user_simulator.text: That works. The plain one is close to what I’d send, and the polished one is fine too. No need to dress it up much.
